summary: This cycle (June 8-9): Anthropic drops the most consequential AI alignment paper of 2026 — "When AI Builds Itself" reveals Claude's coding success rate hit 76% in May 2026, research accuracy jumped from 51% to 64% in 6 months, and agents autonomously solved nearly all of a key safety research challenge. Recursive self-improvement is no longer theoretical. Apple WWDC 2026 delivered: a 3-tier AI architecture for Siri (on-device 3B model → Private Cloud Compute → 1.2T Google Gemini) with Core AI replacing Core ML. OpenAI plans its biggest ChatGPT revamp since 2022 — a superapp combining Codex, AI agents, and third-party integrations ahead of IPO. Google releases Gemma 4 12B, the strongest open local agent model yet, running on 16GB laptops. Microsoft opens Visual Studio's AI layer to outside models with agents embedded in the debugger and profiler. NVIDIA Cosmos 3 brings the first open omnimodal world model for Physical AI. MiniMax M3 is the first open-weights model combining frontier coding, 1M context, and native multimodality. The structural theme: the open-weight agent ecosystem is catching up to closed frontier models at remarkable speed, Anthropic's RSI data reframes the safety conversation, and the platform-level agent infrastructure battle (Apple Siri, OpenAI superapp, Microsoft VS agents, Google local Gemma) is intensifying across every layer of the stack.

highlights.title: Anthropic publishes 'When AI Builds Itself' — recursive self-improvement benchmark data shows Claude improving autonomously
highlights.meta: June 5-9, 2026 · RSI paper · Claude 76% coding success
highlights.why: Anthropic publishes the most consequential AI alignment paper of 2026: Claude's coding success rate hit 76% in May 2026, its next-step accuracy on research tasks grew from 51% (Opus 4.5, Nov 2025) to 64% (Mythos Preview, Apr 2026), and Claude-powered agents autonomously solved nearly all of a key AI safety research challenge. Anthropic warns that recursive self-improvement — AI designing and building its own successor — may arrive within years, not decades. The paper includes the first rigorous benchmark data on how fast AI is accelerating AI R&D.

highlights.title: Apple WWDC 2026 Day 1: Gemini-powered Siri revealed with 3-tier architecture — on-device 3B model + PCC + 1.2T Gemini
highlights.meta: June 8, 2026 · 3-tier Siri · Core AI replaces Core ML · iOS 27
highlights.why: Apple's WWDC keynote delivered: a 3-tier AI architecture — on-device Apple Foundation Models (~3B params) for simple tasks, Private Cloud Compute for medium complexity, and Google Gemini (1.2T custom model, ~$1B/year) for hard problems. Core AI replaces Core ML as the developer framework. Siri gets a dedicated app with conversation history, expressive voice controls, and cross-app reasoning on iOS 27, macOS 27, and visionOS. The 'agent as OS' thesis got its biggest production deployment yet.

highlights.title: OpenAI plans biggest ChatGPT revamp since 2022 — superapp with coding tools, AI agents, and third-party integrations ahead of IPO
highlights.meta: June 7-8, 2026 · Superapp · IPO prep · Codex inside ChatGPT
highlights.why: The Financial Times reports OpenAI is planning the most radical ChatGPT overhaul since its 2022 launch — rebuilding it as a superapp that bundles Codex coding tools, AI agents, and third-party integrations to drive revenue ahead of a planned IPO. Codex capabilities are expected to appear directly within ChatGPT 'in the coming weeks.' This is OpenAI's response to Claude Code's breakout success and the agent economy's rapid maturation.

highlights.title: Google Gemma 4 12B — open multimodal model runs local agent workflows on laptops with 16GB RAM
highlights.why: Google DeepMind's Gemma 4 12B brings agentic, multimodal AI to ordinary laptops with 16GB RAM under Apache 2.0 license. Unified encoder-free architecture handles text, image, audio, and video natively with 256K context. Comes with Google AI Edge tooling for local agent deployment — dynamic Python code execution, offline voice dictation. Nearly matches the twice-as-large 26B model on benchmarks while running entirely on consumer hardware. This is the strongest open-weight local agent model to date.

highlights.title: Microsoft opens Visual Studio AI layer — agents embedded in debugger and profiler, supports outside models
highlights.meta: June 8, 2026 · Multi-model AI · Debugger agents · Profiler agents
highlights.why: Microsoft opens Visual Studio's AI layer to outside models and embeds agents directly into the debugger and profiler — a response to enterprises left behind by Copilot's single-model approach. This lets teams bring their own models (Claude, Gemini, open-source) into the IDE and get agent assistance inside debugging and performance profiling sessions. A major shift in how enterprise IDE agent integration works.

highlights.title: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 — first open omnimodal world foundation model for Physical AI
highlights.why: NVIDIA releases Cosmos 3, the first open 'omnimodal' world foundation model that jointly processes text, image, video, audio, and action for Physical AI — robotics, autonomous vehicles, and embodied agents. Ranked best open-source Text-to-Image and Image-to-Video by Artificial Analysis. This extends the agent model paradigm beyond language into physical world understanding.

highlights.title: MiniMax M3 — first open-weights model to combine frontier coding + 1M context + native multimodality
highlights.meta: June 1, 2026 · Open weights · 1M context · Frontier coding
highlights.why: MiniMax M3 is the first open-weights model to stack frontier-level coding performance, a million-token context window, and native multimodality into a single architecture — capabilities that were previously only available in closed-source frontier models. Together with NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra and Qwen3.7-Plus, the open-weight agent ecosystem now has genuine competition for closed frontier models.

highlights.title: Miasma Worm weaponizes AI coding agents — 73 Microsoft GitHub repos disabled
highlights.why: The first self-replicating worm specifically targeting AI coding agents through their config files (Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code settings). This is the agent ecosystem's Log4j moment — a supply-chain attack vector that affects every team using AI coding agents, across every vendor. 73 repos disabled across 4 Microsoft orgs is just the opening salvo.

highlights.title: AI agent uncovers 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg — cost ~$1,000
highlights.why: Deep engineering posts about how agent loops actually work internally — tool sequencing, context windows, error recovery — are more valuable than most product announcements.

highlights.title: Microsoft Build 2026: MAI-Thinking-1 (1T MoE reasoning model), Agent Framework GA, Windows as agent platform
highlights.meta: June 2, 2026 · 7 in-house models · Agent Framework GA
highlights.why: Microsoft's biggest AI agent infrastructure push ever: MAI-Thinking-1 (1T param MoE, 35B active, trained without OpenAI data) is their first in-house reasoning model, plus 7 total models. Agent Framework GA converges AutoGen + Semantic Kernel into a single commercially supported SDK. Windows Agent Runtime makes Windows an agent platform — not just an app runtime. This is Microsoft's 'full agent stack' moment.

highlights.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra — 550B open MoE with 1M-token context for long-running agents
highlights.summary: How Wasmer used Codex to build a Node.js runtime for the edge
highlights.why: Agent UX is moving beyond chat boxes — interface design is becoming part of the research frontier.

highlights.title: Codex for every role, tool, and workflow
highlights.summary: Codex for every role, tool, and workflow
highlights.why: The strongest practical signal right now is still better tooling around how models call, sequence, and recover from actions.

highlights.title: Show HN: Formally verified polygon intersection – Opus 4.8 oneshots, prev failed
highlights.summary: To my knowledge, this is the first formally verified implementation of an intersection algorithm for polygons. The experience of working with AI agents on this project changed a lot with recent model releases, as I describe in the readme. Opus 4.8 is able to provide algorithm implementation with formal proof in one shot, whereas previous models required me to provide proof strategies in multiple steps. Trust in the correctness comes entirely from the Lean checker and human review of a small specification, not from the LLM. Also check out the web demo built around the verified core linked in the readme: https://schildep.github.io/verified-polygon-intersection/ . It supports multipolygons including holes, self intersections, and overlapping edges.
highlights.why: Opus 4.8 with Dynamic Workflows orchestrating hundreds of sub-agents is the biggest agent-infrastructure release this cycle. Combined with 69.2% SWE-Bench Pro, Fast Mode (3x cheaper), and 41-day release cadence, Anthropic is sprinting on both capability and trustworthiness.

highlights.title: google-gemini/gemini-cli
highlights.summary: An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal.
highlights.why: The local/open-weight ecosystem keeps making serious agent workflows cheaper to run, inspect, and iterate.

highlights.title: LearningCircuit/local-deep-research
highlights.why: OpenAI's first dedicated prompt-injection defense ships just days after the Meta chatbot hack. Lockdown Mode restricts what ChatGPT can be instructed to do, and Elevated Risk labels flag capabilities that could be exploited. This is the industry's first 'seatbelt mandate' moment — product-level security architecture responding to a live attack.

highlights.title: Moltbook AI Agent social network — 900K agents collaborating, database exposed
highlights.why: An AI agent social network — Moltbook — had its entire database exposed, revealing 900K autonomous agents collaborating on tasks across a social graph. The leaked data shows the scale of autonomous agent-to-agent communication already happening in production. Security posture of agent platforms is now a major concern.

highlights.title: CDT Report: 37 dark patterns in ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini — first systematic catalog
highlights.meta: May 2026 · 37 patterns · ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Replika
highlights.why: The Center for Digital Technology published the first systematic catalog of 37 manipulative design patterns embedded across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Replika, and Character.AI. This is the first regulatory-grade taxonomy of dark patterns in AI agent interfaces and will directly shape upcoming AI transparency legislation.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot moves to AI Credits usage-based billing — June 1, 2026
highlights.why: Every Copilot plan moves from flat-fee subscription to usage-based AI Credits billing. Agentic coding consumption is now metered directly — completions stay free, but agentic loops burn credits fast. This pushes developers toward local models and cost-aware agent architectures. Major backlash from the GitHub community on May 30.

highlights.title: Agent browser war goes mainstream — Atlas, Comet, Chrome Auto Browse all GA
highlights.meta: June 2026 · Atlas · Comet · Chrome Auto Browse
highlights.why: The browser itself is becoming the agent interface. ChatGPT Atlas (OpenAI's AI-native browser), Perplexity Comet (now cross-platform GA on iOS, Android, Mac, Windows), and Chrome Auto Browse with Gemini on a two-week release cycle — three major platforms racing to make the browser the primary agent interaction surface. This is a structural shift from search-based to agent-assisted browsing.

highlights.title: AI coding agents now used in 22-28% of GitHub projects — large-scale adoption study
highlights.why: A large-scale academic-style analysis of 128,018 software projects found AI coding-agent adoption rates of 22.20% to 28.66%. This is the first rigorous measurement of how deeply coding agents have penetrated real software development — not a demo or a survey, but actual project-level analysis. Coding agents have crossed the chasm from novelty to infrastructure.

highlights.title: Claude Agent SDK billing split — $200/month credit, June 15 enforcement
highlights.meta: June 2026 · $200/mo Agent SDK credit · June 15 deadline
highlights.why: Starting June 15, Claude Agent SDK and programmatic agent usage come off unlimited. Max subscribers get $200/month in Agent SDK credits; everything beyond that is metered. This is the clearest sign yet that programmatic agent usage is growing fast enough to need its own pricing tier — the agent economy's billing model is emerging in real time.

highlights.title: OpenAI Codex hits 5M+ weekly users, Codex Sites + role-specific plugins roll out
highlights.meta: June 2, 2026 · 6 role plugins · Sites · Enterprise GA
highlights.why: Codex now serves 5M+ weekly users as OpenAI pivots it from developer tool to universal work platform. Role-specific plugins for Engineering, Product, Design, Research, Data Science, and Support. Codex Sites lets non-developers build full apps from prompts. The 'agent as universal interface' bet is officially in production.

highlights.title: Anthropic files confidential S-1 with SEC — IPO signals agent industry maturation
highlights.why: Anthropic going public is the strongest signal that the agent-AI industry is maturing from R&D lab to publicly traded business. An IPO creates financial transparency for the entire supply chain and sets a valuation benchmark for the category.

items.title: Anthropic 'When AI Builds Itself' — recursive self-improvement benchmarks
items.why: Anthropic's most consequential alignment paper: Claude's autonomous coding success rate is 76%, research next-step accuracy grew from 51% to 64% in 6 months, and agents autonomously solved nearly all of a key AI safety challenge. Recursive self-improvement is no longer theoretical.

items.title: Apple WWDC 2026: Gemini-powered Siri with 3-tier architecture — 3B on-device + PCC + 1.2T Gemini
items.why: Apple ships a 3-tier AI architecture: on-device Apple Foundation Models (3B), Private Cloud Compute for mid-complexity, and custom 1.2T Gemini for hard problems. Core AI replaces Core ML. Siri gets a dedicated app. The largest consumer agent deployment.

items.title: Google Gemma 4 12B — local agentic AI on laptops under Apache 2.0
items.why: Open multimodal model running on 16GB laptops. Unified encoder-free architecture handles text, image, audio, video. Google AI Edge tooling enables local agent deployment. Nearly matches 26B model benchmarks.

items.title: OpenAI plans ChatGPT 'superapp' revamp with agents and Codex inside
items.why: Biggest ChatGPT overhaul since 2022: bundling Codex, AI agents, and third-party integrations. IPO-driven strategic pivot to superapp model.

items.title: Microsoft opens Visual Studio AI layer — multi-model agents in debugger and profiler
items.why: VS opens AI layer to outside models (Claude, Gemini, open-source). Agents embedded directly in debugging and performance profiling — enterprise IDE agent integration reaches a new level.

items.title: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 — open omnimodal world foundation model for Physical AI
items.why: First open omnimodal world model handling text, image, video, audio, and action for robotics and Physical AI. Extends agent paradigm beyond language.

items.title: MiniMax M3 — open-weights frontier coding + 1M context + native multimodality
items.why: First open-weights model combining frontier coding, million-token context, and native multimodality. Closed-source-level capabilities in an open package.

items.title: Apple WWDC 2026: Gemini-powered Siri with 1.2T param custom model
items.why: The largest consumer AI agent deployment in history: Siri rebuilt on a custom 1.2T-param Gemini model at ~$1B/year. Always-on persistent agent integrated at the OS level across iOS 27, macOS 27, visionOS.

items.title: Microsoft MAI-Thinking-1 + Agent Framework GA — 7 in-house models, Windows agent platform
items.meta: June 2, 2026 · 1T MoE · Agent Framework GA · 7 models
items.why: Microsoft's full agent-stack push: MAI-Thinking-1 (1T MoE, 35B active, trained without OpenAI data), MAI-Code-1-Flash, Agent Framework GA (AutoGen + Semantic Kernel convergence), and Windows Agent Runtime.

items.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra — 550B open MoE with 1M context for agents
items.why: Open-weight challenger with 1M-token context — hybrid Mamba-2/Transformer architecture delivers 5-6x faster inference than comparable open LLMs. Immediately the strongest open option for long-running agents.

items.title: OpenAI Lockdown Mode — prompt injection protection ships
items.why: First dedicated prompt-injection defense from a major lab. Lockdown Mode restricts instruction-following for sensitive operations. Elevated Risk labels flag exploitable capabilities. Response to the Meta chatbot hack.

items.title: OpenAI Codex hits 5M+ weekly users with role plugins + Sites GA
items.why: 5M+ weekly users, 6 role-specific plugins, Codex Sites for prompt-to-app. The most aggressive expansion of a coding agent's addressable market this year.

items.title: Anthropic S-1 filing — IPO in motion
items.why: Industry-wide maturation signal — Anthropic's IPO sets a valuation benchmark for the entire agent-AI category.

items.title: Claude Agent SDK billing split — $200 credit, June 15 enforcement
items.why: Programmatic agent usage gets its own pricing tier — $200/month Agent SDK credit for Max subscribers, metered beyond. The agent economy's billing model is taking shape.

items.title: Meta Business Agent — Meta enters enterprise AI race
items.why: Meta directly enters enterprise agents — AI for automating business operations across WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram. 1M+ businesses already using earlier versions. Initially free, paid tiers coming.

items.title: Miasma Worm: self-replicating worm targets AI coding agents — 73 Microsoft repos hit
items.why: The agent ecosystem's Log4j moment — a self-replicating worm uses AI coding agent config files (.vscode/tasks.json, Claude Code configs) to spread across GitHub repos, inject 4.3MB droppers, and steal cloud credentials. 73 repos disabled, 286 malicious npm packages across 57 packages.

items.title: AI agent uncovers 21 zero-days in FFmpeg — bug hunting at $1k cost
items.why: Dynamic Workflows is a genuinely new paradigm for agent orchestration — hundreds of parallel sub-agents coordinated by the frontier model itself. This signals that orchestration, not raw model quality, is becoming the competitive moat.

items.summary: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
items.why: Major lab launches still reshape the practical design space for agent builders almost overnight.

tangentRadar.topic: Recursive self-improvement goes from theoretical to benchmarked — Anthropic's RSI data changes the timeline
tangentRadar.whyItMatters: Anthropic's 'When AI Builds Itself' paper is the first to publish rigorous benchmark data on AI accelerating AI R&D: Claude's coding success rate is 76%, next-step research accuracy went from 51% to 64% in 6 months, and agents autonomously solved nearly all of a key AI safety challenge. This isn't speculation — it's measured data showing AI systems are improving the pace of AI research faster than expected. The paper's central claim — that recursive self-improvement (AI designing and building its own successor) may arrive within years, not decades — has sparked the most substantive safety debate since the GPT-4 era. Every agent builder needs to understand this trajectory.
signals: Claude coding success rate: 76% in May 2026 (up from ~50% in late 2025)
Next-step research accuracy: 51% (Opus 4.5, Nov 2025) → 64% (Mythos Preview, Apr 2026)
Claude-powered agents solved nearly all of a key AI safety research challenge autonomously
Anthropic asks rivals to pause — 'AI may soon design and train its own successors'
Forbes, Scientific American, Times of India, Indian Express all cover the paper
HN and Reddit debates: 'is this actually different from automation?' vs 'this is the biggest story of 2026'

tangentRadar.topic: Open-weight agent models reach critical mass — Gemma 4 12B, MiniMax M3, Cosmos 3, Nemotron 3 all land in one week
tangentRadar.whyItMatters: In a single week, the open-weight ecosystem added: Gemma 4 12B (local laptops, Apache 2.0, multimodal), MiniMax M3 (frontier coding + 1M context), NVIDIA Cosmos 3 (omnimodal world model), and NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra (550B MoE, 1M context). This is an unprecedented concentration of capable open models — each targeting a different agent use case. The open-vs-closed gap has narrowed to the point where for specific workloads (local deployment, long context, fine-tuning), open models are now the pragmatic choice, not just the ideological one.
signals: Gemma 4 12B: runs on 16GB laptops, nearly matches 26B benchmarks, Apache 2.0
MiniMax M3: first open-weights model with frontier coding + 1M context + native multimodality
NVIDIA Cosmos 3: first open omnimodal world model for Physical AI
NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra: 550B MoE, 55B active, 1M context, 5-6x faster inference
Reddit testing shows Gemma 4 12B works as local agent on M5 Pro and 4080 Super
Kimi K2.6 already leading open-weights on Artificial Analysis Index (54 points)
Open-weight coding models now competitive with Claude and GPT on specific benchmarks

tangentRadar.topic: The 'agent platform' war escalates — Apple Siri, OpenAI superapp, Microsoft VS, Google Gemma local agents
tangentRadar.whyItMatters: Four major platforms announced agent infrastructure plays in a 48-hour window: Apple's 3-tier Siri architecture (WWDC), OpenAI's ChatGPT superapp plan (FT report), Microsoft opening VS's AI layer to multi-model agents (Build follow-up), and Google's Gemma 4 12B with local agent tooling. The common thread: every platform is converging on the same insight — the OS, the IDE, the browser, and the chat app are all becoming agent runtimes. The differentiation isn't in the model anymore; it's in the integration depth, the tool ecosystem, and the trust model. The agent platform war has officially begun.
signals: Apple WWDC: 3-tier Siri architecture, Core AI replaces Core ML, iOS 27
OpenAI FT report: biggest ChatGPT revamp since 2022, superapp with Codex + agents
Microsoft VS: multi-model AI layer, agents in debugger + profiler
Google Gemma 4 12B: local agent deployment on laptops with Google AI Edge
CDT: 37 dark patterns catalogued across all major agent platforms
GitHub Copilot AI Credits: usage-based billing for agentic coding from June 1

tangentRadar.topic: Agent supply chain security — the Miasma worm changes everything
tangentRadar.whyItMatters: The strongest practical signal right now is still better tooling around how models call, sequence, and recover from actions.
signals: OpenAI: Harness engineering: Leveraging Codex in an agent-first world

tangentRadar.topic: NVIDIA enters the open-weight agent model race — Nemotron 3 Ultra
tangentRadar.whyItMatters: NVIDIA's 550B open MoE model with 1M-token context and hybrid Mamba-2/Transformer architecture is purpose-built for long-running agent tasks. This is the strongest open-weight option for agents needing long context — RAG, multi-turn code generation, document-level reasoning. Combined with Qwen3.7-Plus and Mistral Medium 3.5, the open-weight agent ecosystem now has legitimate competition for closed frontier models. The gap between open and closed is narrowing for specific use cases (long context, local deployment, fine-tuning).
signals: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra: 550B MoE, 55B active, 1M context
Hybrid Mamba-2/Transformer — 5-6x faster inference than comparable open LLMs
Released on Hugging Face, ModelScope, OpenRouter
Qwen3.7-Plus: vision + tool invocation for agents
Mistral Medium 3.5: 77.6% SWE-Bench Verified, 256K context
local-deep-research: ~95% SimpleQA on a single 3090

tangentRadar.topic: OpenAI Codex transitions from developer tool to universal work platform
tangentRadar.whyItMatters: 5M+ weekly users, six role-specific plugins (Engineering, Product, Design, Research, Data Science, Support), Codex Sites for prompt-to-app, and GA on AWS Bedrock — Codex is no longer a coding agent. It's OpenAI's bet on the 'agent as universal interface' for all knowledge workers. The key tension: role plugins vs the 'natural language programming' skepticism from HN. Early data (15-minute CRUD apps, 5M weekly users) suggests adoption is real, but long-term maintenance and iteration workflows remain unproven.
signals: 5M+ weekly users — up from ~2M in March 2026
6 role-specific plugins launched at 'Intelligence at Work' event
Codex Sites: build full apps from natural language prompts
Enterprise GA on Amazon Bedrock with Managed Agents
VentureBeat: 'Codex lets agents build interactive enterprise workspaces'
Reddit validation: full SaaS app in one afternoon
HN skepticism: 'prompt engineering is still a skill barrier'

experimentQueue.title: Reproduce Anthropic's RSI findings — measure how fast an agent improves its own coding ability
experimentQueue.why: Anthropic's data showing 76% coding success rate and 64% next-step research accuracy is the most important empirical finding this cycle. Reproducing this on a different model family (Qwen, Nemotron, or GPT-5.5) would validate whether RSI is model-specific or a general phenomenon.
experimentQueue.prompt: Design an experiment to measure 'self-improvement velocity': 1) Have an agent attempt a set of N coding tasks, 2) Feed the successful trajectories back as in-context examples, 3) Measure the improvement rate over 5-10 iterations. Compare across Claude, GPT-5.5, Qwen3.7-Plus, and Nemotron 3 Ultra. Does the rate of improvement accelerate, and does it differ by model family?

experimentQueue.title: Test Gemma 4 12B as a local agent backend against cloud Claude/Codex on real workflows
experimentQueue.why: Gemma 4 12B claims near-26B performance on 16GB laptops. A head-to-head against cloud Claude and Codex on realistic agent workflows (multi-file refactoring, web research, data analysis) would reveal the true local-vs-cloud agent quality gap today.
experimentQueue.prompt: Set up Gemma 4 12B via Ollama/LM Studio on a Mac with 16GB+ RAM. Build 5 realistic agent workflows (code migration, research synthesis, data pipeline, API integration, bug hunt). Run each with Gemma 4 12B (local), Claude Code (cloud), and Codex (cloud). Measure: completion rate, quality score, time-to-complete, cost-per-task, and human correction effort. Publish as a benchmark.

experimentQueue.title: Build an agent that uses the new 3-tier Apple Siri architecture (simulated)
experimentQueue.why: Apple's 3-tier Siri architecture (on-device → PCC → Gemini) is the most complex agent routing system ever deployed. Simulating this routing pattern with open models could reveal best practices for tiered agent compute.
experimentQueue.prompt: Design a 3-tier agent architecture mimicking Apple's: Tier 1 = local 3B model (Gemma 4 12B Q4 quantized as stand-in), Tier 2 = cloud small model, Tier 3 = frontier API. Build a router that classifies task complexity and routes accordingly. Measure cost savings vs always-using-frontier, latency distribution, and user satisfaction per tier. Document the routing logic and failure modes.

experimentQueue.title: Simulate the OpenAI superapp — build a multi-agent workspace with Codex-style tools + third-party integrations
experimentQueue.why: OpenAI's superapp vision combines coding agents, AI agents, and third-party integrations. Building a prototype reveals what's real vs vaporware and what integration challenges exist.
experimentQueue.prompt: Design and build a mini 'superapp' workspace that integrates: 1) a coding agent (using Claude Code or Codex API), 2) a research agent (web search + synthesis), 3) a data analysis agent (Python + visualization), 4) third-party API integrations (email, calendar, CRM). Use MCP for tool connectivity. Measure: cross-agent context sharing quality, user switching cost, and total task completion time vs using separate tools.
